economic_notes: First Nation operates a store enterprise, agricultural operations (cattle herd of 145 animals), limited partnerships in development and property, and TLE land holding activities. Heritage Trust fund maintained. Capital projects include midwifery project, birthing center, west subdivision, lagoon upgrades, and school roof repairs.
notes: Comparative 2023 figures restated per Note 30. Province is Saskatchewan (auditor based in Saskatoon, SK). Four consolidated entities and three GBEs accounted for under modified equity method. Net financial position is a net debt of $666,570. Unexpended revenue (deferred revenue) of $22.6M is significant. Large capital asset additions of $17.3M in year primarily assets under construction. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $44,931,724 + PAGC $1,692,176 + First Nations Trust $1,477,356 + CMHC $1,044,163 + SIIT $241,735 + Ottawa Trust $621,575 + Indigenous Languages & Culture $516,040 + TLE Revenue $281,155 + Northern Lights $208,916 = $51,014,840 (rounded to $51,014,644 per line items). OSR includes store sales, other revenue, admin fees, LP earnings, rental income, GBE earnings, and deferral recognition.
